# Pre-Work Check

Cross-checks proposed work against the auto-generated work index so principals never authorize duplicate, conflicting, or already-shipped work.

## When to Use

Load this skill when:
- A new idea is being triaged into the backlog
- A new spec is being drafted or approved
- A sprint is being planned or a task is being dispatched
- A worker reports back asking to expand scope

Do NOT load when:
- Fixing a typo, formatting nit, or other trivial change with no scope impact
- Working strictly inside a task that already passed this gate

## Inputs

- `spec-driven-development/exec/work-index.md` (auto-generated by `cli/state_builder.py`)
  - Section 1: DONE features (do not re-implement)
  - Section 2: IN-FLIGHT features (coordinate before touching)
  - Section 3: QUEUED backlog items (already captured)
- The proposed work item (title, intent, files likely touched)

## Process

1. Read `spec-driven-development/exec/work-index.md` end-to-end.
2. Compare the proposed work against each section:
   - DONE: is the proposed work already shipped? If yes, STOP and route back as a follow-up or amendment to the existing feature.
   - IN-FLIGHT: does the proposed work touch the same files, modules, or behaviors? If yes, coordinate with the owning feature before dispatch.
   - QUEUED: is this already in the backlog under a different title? If yes, link instead of duplicating.
3. Confirm the three pre-work assertions before approving:
   1. Not a duplicate of DONE
   2. Not a conflict with IN-FLIGHT
   3. Not a duplicate of QUEUED
4. If any assertion fails, document the conflict and route the item back to triage or to the owning principal.
5. If the work-index is stale (older than the latest spec or sprint event), regenerate it with `python spec-driven-development/cli/state_builder.py` before deciding.

## Output

- Either an explicit approval citing the work-index sections that were cleared,
- Or a block with the conflicting entry (DONE / IN-FLIGHT / QUEUED) named.

## Common Mistakes

- Skipping the check because the proposal "sounds new" -- always read the index.
- Approving against a stale index. Regenerate when in doubt.
- Treating a DONE match as a green light to "redo it better" -- raise an amendment spec instead.
- Letting an IN-FLIGHT collision turn into parallel edits on the same files.
